results.question: Why did Chipotle's labor costs change?

claims.claim: Labor costs decreased 0.2% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2024 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2023, including 1.5% from sales leverage, partially offset by 1.1% due to restaurant wage inflation, of which 0.5% was due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

Labor costs decreased 0.3% as a percentage of total revenue for the six months ended June 30, 2024 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2023, including 1.3% from sales leverage, partially offset by 0.8% due to restaurant wage inflation, of which 0.3% was due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

Labor costs decreased 0.4% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2025 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2024, including 1.2% from sales leverage, partially offset by 0.8% due to restaurant wage inflation, of which 0.4% was due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

Labor costs decreased 0.3% as a percentage of total revenue for the six months ended June 30, 2025 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2024, including 1.2% from sales leverage, partially offset by 0.9% due to restaurant wage inflation, of which 0.4% was due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

How did wage inflation affect Chipotle's labor costs?

claims.claim: Labor costs decreased 0.2% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2024, including 1.1% due to restaurant wage inflation, of which 0.5% was due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

Labor costs increased 0.6% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2025, due to the impact from lower sales volumes. A 0.5% benefit from menu price increases in the prior year and efficient management of labor more than offset wage inflation.

Labor costs increased 1.1% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026, primarily driven by a 0.4% impact from costs related to certain legal proceedings, 0.3% from wage inflation, 0.3% from lower average restaurant sales volumes, and 0.2% from higher benefits expense, including performance-based bonuses. These increases were partially offset by a 0.2% benefit from menu price increases.

Labor costs increased 0.6% as a percentage of total revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2025, due to the impact from lower sales volumes, as a 0.7% benefit from menu price increases in the prior year was offset by restaurant wage inflation, including minimum wage increases for our restaurants in California.

results.question: Why did Cracker Barrel's operating costs change?

claims.claim: During the second quarter and first six months of 2025, Cracker Barrel recorded impairment charges of $2,163 and $2,863, respectively, as a result of the deterioration in operating performance in three MSBC locations and two Cracker Barrel locations. Additionally, one Cracker Barrel store was closed in the second quarter and first six months of 2025 resulting in closing costs of $288. No stores were closed in the second quarter or first six months of 2024.

The decrease in professional fees as a percentage of total revenue in the third quarter of 2025 as compared to the same period in the prior year resulted primarily from lower costs related to the Company’s strategic transformation plan.

The decrease in incentive compensation expense as a percentage of total revenue in the third quarter of 2025 as compared to the same period in the prior year resulted primarily from Chief Executive Officer transition costs incurred in 2024.
The increase in other store operating expenses as a percentage of total revenue in 2025 as compared to 2024 resulted primarily from costs associated with the Company’s off-premise business.

The decrease in operating income (loss) in the first quarter of 2025 as compared to the same period in the prior year resulted primarily from the decreases in total revenue partially offset by lower cost of goods sold expenses, lower labor expenses, lower incentive compensation expense and lower professional fees. Additionally, for the first six months of 2025, higher media advertising and higher maintenance expenses further contributed to the operating loss in 2025.

results.question: How did Chipotle's restaurant development and new openings evolve?

claims.claim: During the three months ended June 30, 2024, Chipotle opened 52 company-operated restaurants, which included 46 restaurants with a Chipotlane.
The company is on track to open approximately 285-315 new restaurants in 2024.
It expects that at least 80% of its new restaurants will include a Chipotlane.
During the three months ended September 30, 2024, Chipotle opened 86 company-operated restaurants, which included 73 restaurants with a Chipotlane.
The company is on track to open approximately 285 to 315 new company-operated restaurants in 2024.
It expects that at least 80% of its new company-operated restaurants will include a Chipotlane.
During the three months ended March 31, 2025, Chipotle opened 57 restaurants, which included 48 restaurants with a Chipotlane.
The company expects to open approximately 315 to 345 company-owned restaurants in 2025.
It expects that at least 80% of its new company-owned restaurants will include a Chipotlane.
During the three months ended September 30, 2025, Chipotle opened 84 restaurants, which included 64 restaurants with a Chipotlane.
The company remains on track to open approximately 315 to 345 company-owned restaurants in 2025.
It expects to open approximately 350 to 370 restaurants in 2026, which includes 10 to 15 international partner-operated restaurants.
It expects that at least 80% of its new company-owned restaurants will include a Chipotlane.

results.question: How did Olive Garden's segment results perform?

For the first nine months of fiscal 2026, Olive Garden's segment profit margin remained flat at 21.8% compared to 21.8% in the same period of fiscal 2025.

results.question: How did Darden's acquisition of Chuy's impact financial results?

claims.claim: The acquisition of Chuy’s in fiscal 2025 had a significant impact on Darden’s financial results.
Depreciation and amortization expenses increased as a percent of sales primarily due to the acquisition of Chuy’s as well as incremental depreciation on new restaurants and other capital investments.
The acquisition of Chuy’s in fiscal 2025 also impacted the company’s outlook for fiscal 2025.
Darden expected sales for fiscal 2025 to be approximately $12.1 billion, with same-restaurant sales growth of approximately 1.5 percent and approximately 50 to 55 new restaurant openings.

The company expected capital expenditures to be approximately $650 million, which included the addition of Chuy’s and the company’s expectations for Chuy’s results from the date of acquisition forward.
The impact of the Chuy’s acquisition continued into fiscal 2026.

Darden expected sales growth for fiscal 2026 to be approximately 9.5 percent, driven by growth of approximately 2.0 percent related to the fifty-third week in fiscal 2026; same-restaurant sales growth to be approximately 4.5 percent; and new restaurant openings to be approximately 70.

The company expected capital expenditures to be between $750 and $775 million, which included the addition of Chuy’s and the company’s expectations for Chuy’s results from the date of acquisition forward.
The acquisition was expected to be completed in the second quarter of fiscal 2025 and would be funded through one or more new debt issuances.

results.question: What is Cracker Barrel's business model and store concept?

claims.claim: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. is a publicly traded company that operates and develops the Cracker Barrel Old Country Store® concept.
Each Cracker Barrel store consists of a restaurant with a gift shop.
The restaurants serve breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
The gift shop offers a variety of decorative and functional items specializing in rocking chairs, holiday gifts, toys, apparel, and foods.
The company also operates the Maple Street Biscuit Company (MSBC), a breakfast and lunch fast casual concept, which has 68 locations as of September 12, 2025.

results.question: How did Cracker Barrel's general and administrative expenses change?

claims.claim: General and administrative expenses increased from 5.0% of total revenue in 2023 to 6.0% in 2024. The increase was primarily driven by higher professional fees, payroll and related expenses, and incentive compensation expense.

General and administrative expenses increased from 6.0% of total revenue in 2024 to 6.5% in the first quarter of 2025. The increase was primarily driven by higher professional fees related to legal fees and proxy contest expenses, as well as higher costs associated with the Company’s strategic transformation plan. Additionally, the Company incurred higher costs of approximately $2,300 related to the strategic transformation plan for the first six months of 2025.

General and administrative expenses increased from 6.0% of total revenue in 2024 to 6.2% in the first six months of 2025. The increase was primarily driven by higher professional fees and incentive compensation expense. The increase in professional fees was due to proxy contest expenses and higher legal fees, while the increase in incentive compensation expense was due to higher bonus payouts.

General and administrative expenses decreased from 6.0% of total revenue in the first quarter of 2025 to 6.0% in the first quarter of 2026. The decrease was primarily driven by a decrease in incentive compensation expense and a decrease in professional fees. The decrease in incentive compensation expense was due to lower performance against financial objectives in 2026. The decrease in professional fees was due to lower legal fees and lower proxy contest expenses.
